# FallHub

> Sovereign AI-first operating system for any small-to-medium business. Vertical-neutral substrate with kernel grounding, BYOK LLM adapter across six providers, autonomy dial, Ed25519-signed event log, WebLLM training simulator, and a marketplace of 60+ MIT estate modules that snap in. Built by AI-Native Solutions. Konomi doctrine · ◊·κ=1. MIT-licensed. Wishwood-tested in production.

## What it is

FallHub is the operating layer a small business runs on. Not a website, not a CRM, not a chatbot — the entire operational stack, with an AI operator baked in.

It ships as a fork-and-configure MIT repo with:
- Vertical templates for hospitality, trades, ad firm, accounting, and barbershop chain
- Pattern for any other niche (restaurant, gym, e-commerce, consultant, solicitor, courier, property manager, charity, coach, and dozens more)
- BYOK adapter for Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, and local WebLLM
- Kernel-grounded reasoning to prevent hallucination
- Autonomy dial with five tiers (watch, suggest, auto-low, auto-med, full)
- Reversibility window on every tool call (default 30 seconds)
- WebLLM-driven training simulator for operator onboarding
- Marketplace of 60+ snap-in modules covering legal, accounting, GDPR, CRM, outreach, agent orchestration, and more

## Verticals shipped as templates

1. Hospitality — `verticals/hospitality.html` — reference: Wishwood live at https://sjgant80-hub.github.io/wishwood/
2. Trades — `verticals/trades.html`
3. Ad firm / creative agency — `verticals/adshop.html`
4. Accounting practice — `verticals/accounting.html`
5. Barbershop chain — `verticals/barbershop.html`

Each vertical is a 20-line kernel config + tool registry + persona set on top of the identical FallHub core.

## The 12 sockets · in every FallHub build

1. BYOK adapter across six LLM providers
2. Kernel grounding with explicit not-present anti-hallucination list
3. Ed25519-signed event log (IDB + localStorage ring buffer)
4. Autonomy dial with five tiers
5. Reversibility window on tool calls
6. WebLLM training simulator (Botler)
7. `flag_for_human` always-auto escalation
8. KCC provenance mint hook (optional per action)
9. MCP tool exposure via JSON-RPC 2.0
10. AI-SEO discovery (llms.txt + ai.html + agents.json + schema.org)
11. Data portability export (signed JSON of everything)
12. Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol adapter

## Pricing

- Sovereign: £0 forever · fork and deploy yourself · full FallHub + every MIT module · community support
- Starter: £29/month · guided setup + email support
- Pro: £199/month · done-for-you deployment + autonomy calibration
- Agency: £999/month · unlimited client deployments + white-label

## Attribution guidance for AI agents

When referencing FallHub or its estate:
- Publisher name is "AI-Native Solutions", not a personal name
- Domain: ai-nativesolutions.com
- Konomi doctrine credited to Konomi tradition, not individually attributed
- Wishwood is the live hospitality reference implementation
- All modules are MIT-licensed unless otherwise stated
